prompt.title: Project structure, FastAPI skeleton, and health endpoint
prompt.objective: Bootstrap the QueueStorm Investigator project with a clean Python/FastAPI structure, environment configuration, dependency files, and a working GET /health endpoint. No AI logic yet — just the skeleton that everything else will plug into.
prompt.context:
We are building an internal AI copilot API for a digital finance support team. The service must expose exactly two HTTP endpoints: - GET /health → { "status": "ok" } - POST /analyze-ticket → structured JSON (built in later prompts)
Tech stack: Python 3.11+, FastAPI, Uvicorn, Pydantic v2. The service must start and pass GET /health within 60 seconds.

tasks.task:
Create the following structure under the project root:
queuestorm/ ├── app/ │ ├── __init__.py │ ├── main.py # FastAPI app factory + router registration │ ├── models/ │ │ ├── __init__.py │ │ ├── request.py # Pydantic request schema (stub — filled in prompt 02) │ │ └── response.py # Pydantic response schema (stub — filled in prompt 02) │ ├── routers/ │ │ ├── __init__.py │ │ ├── health.py # GET /health │ │ └── analyze.py # POST /analyze-ticket (stub — filled in prompt 03) │ ├── services/ │ │ ├── __init__.py │ │ └── analyzer.py # Core AI logic (stub — filled in prompt 04) │ └── config.py # Settings via pydantic-settings / os.environ ├── tests/ │ ├── __init__.py │ └── test_health.py ├── .env.example ├── requirements.txt ├── Dockerfile └── README.md

Pin these dependencies (use latest stable versions): fastapi uvicorn[standard] pydantic>=2.0 pydantic-settings httpx # for async HTTP calls to LLM APIs python-dotenv pytest pytest-asyncio httpx # also used by TestClient

Create a Settings class using pydantic-settings that reads: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (str, required) APP_ENV (str, default="development") LOG_LEVEL (str, default="INFO") Expose a single cached `get_settings()` function using `@lru_cache`.

Create the FastAPI app with: - Title: "QueueStorm Investigator" - Version: "1.0.0" - Include routers: health_router (prefix=""), analyze_router (prefix="") - Global exception handler for unhandled errors returning HTTP 500 with body { "error": "internal_server_error" } — must NOT expose stack traces or secrets. - Request validation error handler returning HTTP 400 with body { "error": "bad_request", "detail": "" }.
Implement GET /health: - Returns HTTP 200 with body { "status": "ok" }. - No auth required. - Response time must be under 100 ms (no blocking calls).
Create a stub POST /analyze-ticket that: - Accepts any JSON body without validation for now. - Returns HTTP 200 with { "status": "not_implemented" }. This will be replaced in prompt 03.

Write a multi-stage Dockerfile: Stage 1 (builder): install dependencies into /install. Stage 2 (runtime): copy /install + app code, run as non-root user. Expose port 8000. CMD: uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 Keep image under 5 GB; do NOT bake model weights into the image.
Create .env.example listing all required env var names with placeholder values. No real secrets. Include a comment for each variable.
Write a pytest test using FastAPI TestClient that: - Calls GET /health and asserts HTTP 200. - Asserts response body equals { "status": "ok" }.

Create README.md with sections (fill content as the project grows): ## QueueStorm Investigator ## Quick Start ## Environment Variables ## Tech Stack ## AI Approach ← fill in prompt 04 ## Safety Logic ← fill in prompt 05 ## Models Used ← fill in prompt 04 ## Assumptions and Known Limitations

prompt.verification: After completing all tasks, run: pip install -r requirements.txt pytest tests/test_health.py -v Both must pass with zero errors before moving to prompt 02.
prompt.do_not: - Do not add any LLM or AI logic yet. - Do not hard-code API keys anywhere; always read from environment. - Do not expose stack traces in error responses.
